compiled_state.title: create-dso-app.sh: prerequisite check -> user instructed to install manually rather than auto-installing
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
create-dso-app.sh checks for prerequisites (e.g., Node.js, Python, git) and prints instructions telling the user to install them manually rather than installing them automatically as part of the script.

## Impact
Host project setup is blocked until the user manually installs prerequisites, increasing friction during onboarding and deviating from the expected behavior of a setup script that should handle its own dependencies.

## Expected vs Observed
- **Expected**: create-dso-app.sh detects missing prerequisites and installs them automatically (or offers to do so) before continuing setup.
- **Observed**: create-dso-app.sh detects missing prerequisites and prints a message instructing the user to install them manually, then exits or halts.

## Affected Component
plugins/dso/scripts/create-dso-app.sh — prerequisite check and installation logic

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run create-dso-app.sh on a system missing one or more prerequisites (e.g., Node.js not installed)
2. Observe that the script prints instructions to install the missing tool rather than installing it

## Notes
Reported by user during interactive session. This is a host project UX issue — the script should behave like standard setup scripts (Homebrew, npm create, etc.) that handle their own prerequisites.

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed. Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC tier). severity=1, complexity=1, environment=0, intermittent=0
